symptomSummary: Evolve feature ignores recent feedback after ~30 invocations, generating revisions that address old problems instead of current issues.
rootCauseHypothesis: The repository query fetching invocation feedback likely retrieves all historical feedback without proper recency ordering or limiting, causing older feedback to dilute or overshadow recent issues as the dataset grows.
fixHypothesis: Add ORDER BY timestamp DESC and LIMIT clauses to feedback queries, or implement exponential decay weighting that prioritizes recent invocations over historical ones.

primaryLocation.rationale: Repository layer queries invocation data for evolution. A query without recency ordering or limits would cause exactly this symptom: works fine initially, degrades as feedback accumulates. The file was recently modified, suggesting active work in this area.

candidateLocations.rationale: Evolution service orchestrates how feedback is aggregated and processed into new revisions. If it treats all feedback equally rather than weighting recent items, old problems would persist.
Agent constructs prompts from feedback. The prompt construction may not emphasize or prioritize recent feedback in the context sent to the LLM.
API endpoint that triggers evolution. May not pass parameters to limit or weight feedback by recency when calling the evolution service.
